meaning: vt.使成化石,使陈腐vi.变成化石,搜集(或发掘)化石标本
examples: The tissue and cartilage of a nose are too soft to fossilize.
One interpretation regarding the absence of fossils during this important 100-million-year period is that early animals were soft bodied and simply did not fossilize.
Only a vanishingly small fraction of prehistoric animals died in the perfect conditions--including a swift cover of sediment--necessary to fossilize bones in the first place.
